mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive 20-day returns occur in fewer than 50% of declining rate periods.

mechanism_narrative: A sudden increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.5 percentage points in a single month signals labor market deterioration, reducing consumer spending expectations and causing negative S&P 500 returns over the following month.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE crosses above prior month by > 0.5pp
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 65% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer and business demand growth, which sustains corporate earnings momentum and generates positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in RSAFS and HOUST fails to produce positive 60-day returns in more than 55% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in the index of industrial production below its 12-month moving average signals supply-side weakness that immediately triggers risk-off positioning, resulting in negative S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Weekly returns are positive in more than 60% of instances when INDPRO falls below its moving average.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained CPIAUCSL above 3.0 triggers expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day returns are positive in more than 60% of high-inflation observations.

mechanism_narrative: Steady month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects expanding economic capacity and wage growth, which supports corporate revenue forecasts and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.01 over rolling 3-year windows or positive returns occur in fewer than 50% of growth periods.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between quarterly GDP growth and monthly retail sales, where retail sales accelerate while GDP decelerates, signals a shift toward consumer-driven momentum that outperforms broad economic indicators, yielding positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The divergence pattern fails to predict positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous drop in industrial production and housing starts below their respective 6-month averages indicates a broad-based demand shock to the supply chain, triggering immediate sell-offs and negative S&P 500 returns over the next week.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO and HOUST both cross below 6-month moving averages
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in more than 60% of instances when both series cross below their moving averages.
